name: Icteriidae
description: Icteriidae is a monotypic family of passerine bird found across North America. The single species is a large, secretive warbler-like bird known for its bizarre and varied vocalizations, mimicking other birds and mechanical sounds.

children.name: Icteria
children.description: A monotypic genus of large, thick-billed warbler found in dense thickets across North America.

children.name: Icteria virens
children.description: The Yellow-breasted Chat is a large, olive-green bird with a bright yellow breast, white spectacles, and a heavy bill. It skulks in dense brushy thickets and is more often heard than seen, producing a remarkable repertoire of whistles, cackles, and mimicry.
